name: Births: Gestation vs Weight
description: Gestation weeks vs birth weight (lbs) for US babies, 2014. IMS Ch. 24.
studyDescription: A random sample of births recorded by the US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention in 2014. Each record includes the gestation period and birth weight. This is an observational dataset drawn from vital statistics records.

variableDescriptions.weeks: Length of the pregnancy in completed weeks (gestation period)
variableDescriptions.weight: Birth weight of the baby in pounds

sourceDetail: OpenIntro IMS Ch. 24, openintro::births14. Data from the CDC National Vital Statistics System.

inferenceContexts.parameter: the population slope of the relationship between gestation weeks and birth weight
inferenceContexts.claim: there is a positive linear relationship between gestation length and birth weight
